# RotorPy oracle traces (P1-7)

Committed CSVs generated by `scripts/oracle/export_rotorpy.py` (RotorPy
2.1.2, DOP853 rtol=1e-10) from the `interceptor_quad` airframe, drag-free
matched-parameter config (scoping rationale in the exporter docstring:
drag model forms differ by design and are pinned by unit tests; the oracle
pins the quaternion 6DOF core, rotor thrust/moment allocation and
gravity). The first-order motor lag (tau_m=0.05 s) is a shared harness
construct on both sides — production `MotorEsc` is NOT oracle-pinned; it
is pinned in `tests/test_motor_battery.py`.

Consumed by `tests/test_oracle_rotorpy.py` (`pytest -m oracle`).

## Gates (sanctioned tightening, 2026-06-11)

Old gates pos RMSE < 0.5 m / att RMSE < 3 deg were 2,600x-30,000x looser
than the recorded margins and would have passed constant-level errors
(e.g. a 9.80665-vs-9.81 gravity mix-up gives only ~0.075 m pos RMSE).
Both sides are deterministic (committed DOP853 traces vs our fixed-step
800 Hz RK4), so the gates were tightened to ~25x the worst measured
margin, rounded up to clean numbers:

| gate     | old   | new       | worst measured | headroom |
|----------|-------|-----------|----------------|----------|
| pos RMSE | 0.5 m | 0.005 m   | 1.91e-4 m      | 26x      |
| att RMSE | 3 deg | 0.01 deg  | 8.9e-5 deg     | 112x     |

Margins measured at the 2026-06-11 re-baseline (replay of each committed
trace through `tests/test_oracle_rotorpy.py::replay`):

| flight         | pos RMSE [m] | att RMSE [deg] | min z [m] |
|----------------|--------------|----------------|-----------|
| hover_hold     | 1.74e-4      | 0.0            | 50.0      |
| climb_step     | 1.67e-4      | 0.0            | 50.0      |
| tilt_dash      | 1.91e-4      | 0.0            | 33.3      |
| yaw_spin       | 1.74e-4      | 5.7e-5         | 50.0      |
| pitch_pulse    | 1.74e-4      | 8.9e-5         | 49.3      |
| roll_mix_pulse | 1.74e-4      | 5.7e-5         | 50.0      |

`roll_mix_pulse` (added 2026-06-11) is the only flight that exercises the
Euler coupling term (omega x J omega != 0; peak 0.035 N m, ~8% of the
peak roll torque, nonzero in 600/1001 rows) and the roll allocation
channel tau_x — all other flights are zero- or single-axis rotations with
diagonal inertia, where the term vanishes identically. A fixture-integrity
test pins this property against future re-baselines.

## Known residual: ground effect stays active in the replay

The replay zeroes drag but leaves our Cheeseman-Bennett ground-effect
model active, while RotorPy has no ground-effect model at all. At z=50 m
the gain is 1 + 7.9e-7 (the [1, max_gain] clip does not remove it), a
systematic +0.8 ppm thrust bias that integrates to ~1e-4 m over 10 s —
the dominant residual behind the ~1.9e-4 m margins (with gain forced
to 1 the replay agrees to <1e-7 m). Consequence: **flights must stay at
z >= ~50 m** (GE-vs-no-GE diverges hard below z ~ 0.08 m and the bias
grows as 1/z^2); keep this in mind when adding flights, and do not
tighten the pos gate below the GE floor without neutralizing GE in
`dragfree_params()`.

RotorPy is an **offline oracle only** — installed in the dev venv
(`pip install rotorpy`), never a runtime dependency. Re-running the
exporter overwrites these traces; treat that as a sanctioned re-baseline
(same policy as golden pins). Pass flight names as CLI args to re-record
a subset (e.g. `python scripts/oracle/export_rotorpy.py roll_mix_pulse`).

# ArduPilot SITL oracle trace (P3-9)

`ardupilot_square.json` — the official prebuilt ArduCopter stable SITL
(EKF3, default `+` quad model) flying the same 200 m / 50 m AGL / 10 m/s
waypoint square the P3-8 bench flies. Generated by
`scripts/oracle/export_ardupilot_square.py`, consumed by
`tests/test_oracle_ardupilot.py` (`pytest -m oracle`).

This is an **ENVELOPE cross-check of two complete independent
autopilots on different airframes** (12 kg interceptor vs ~2 kg SITL
quad — different controllers, nav filters, guidance), NOT a model
match: bands assert flight-class agreement (lap-time ratio in
[0.5, 2.0], leg cross-track same class, cruise speed within 30%,
altitude band ±4 m). Recorded 2026-06-12: oracle cross-track 0.67 m /
lap 38 s; bench 1.81 m / 35 s (the bench flies real GNSS GM wander with
plain velocity-vector guidance; ArduPilot S-curve path-follows on
SITL-grade nav).

## One-time WSL2 setup (offline oracle only, never a runtime dep)

```
wsl -d Ubuntu -- bash -lc "mkdir -p ~/apsitl && cd ~/apsitl \
  && curl -fsSL -o arducopter \
     https://firmware.ardupilot.org/Copter/stable/SITL_x86_64_linux_gnu/arducopter \
  && curl -fsSL -o copter.parm \
     https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ArduPilot/ardupilot/master/Tools/autotest/default_params/copter.parm \
  && chmod +x arducopter"
pip install pymavlink   # dev venv
```

Re-record (sanctioned re-baseline, same policy as the RotorPy traces):
`python scripts/oracle/export_ardupilot_square.py` — launches SITL in
WSL2 at 8x speedup, uploads the mission, records GLOBAL_POSITION_INT at
10 Hz until the last waypoint, overwrites the JSON, kills SITL.
